report.title: `..` middleware-inheritance codegen hardcoded the `APP` ident, ignoring the parsed `UrlExpr::app` and silently binding to the wrong global in multi-app setups
report.summary: When an `endpoint!` / `outpoint!` invocation used the `..` middleware-inheritance marker, the proc-macro emitted `APP.handler.get_protocol_middlewares::<Protocol>()` regardless of which app ident the user actually wrote on the URL form. Users who set up a custom server such as `MY_APP.url("/foo")` and listed `..` in `middleware` got middleware inherited from a global called `APP` (which they did not necessarily declare), not from `MY_APP`. With no `APP` symbol in scope this produced a confusing `cannot find value APP in this scope` error far from the macro call; with both `APP` and `MY_APP` in scope it silently inherited the wrong set.

impact: Any project running more than one app (e.g. a primary `APP` plus an admin `ADMIN_APP`, or a single non-default-named server like `MY_APP`) that used `..` to inherit protocol-root middleware on `MY_APP.url("/foo")` got middleware from the wrong server, or a hard compile error blaming the user's site. Middleware inheritance is the documented mechanism for layering auth / CORS / tracing onto routes; binding to the wrong server silently turns route-level security off (or applies the wrong policy). The bug is structural — every `..` site in a non-`APP`-named server is affected. Single-app users hit no symptom because their app *is* `APP` and the hardcoded ident matched by coincidence.

behavior_diff.expected: For `endpoint!(MY_APP.url("/foo"), middleware = [..], fn handler<HTTP> { ... })` the generated inheritance block reads protocol middleware from `MY_APP`, matching the app the URL was registered against. The codegen should use whichever ident was parsed by `UrlExpr` (either the user-written ident or the documented `APP` default for bare-literal forms).
behavior_diff.actual: `hotaru_trans/src/url/urlargs.rs:268` emitted `Ident::new("APP", Span::call_site())` unconditionally. Generated body: `let protocol_middlewares = APP.handler.get_protocol_middlewares::<Protocol>(); middlewares.extend(protocol_middlewares);`. For `MY_APP.url(...)` this reaches for the wrong symbol; the URL itself is registered on `MY_APP` via `UrlExpr::expand` (which correctly uses `self.app.clone()` at urlexpr.rs:125), so registration succeeds but inheritance is misdirected.

status.fix: Two-line change in `hotaru_trans`: (1) added a `pub fn app(&self) -> &Ident` accessor to `UrlExpr` in `src/url/urlexpr.rs` exposing the already-parsed app ident; (2) replaced the hardcoded `Ident::new("APP", Span::call_site())` at `src/url/urlargs.rs:268` with `self.url_expr.app().clone()`. The `APP` fallback for bare-literal URL forms (`endpoint!("/foo", ...)`) is preserved because `UrlExpr::from_tokens` already stores `Ident::new("APP", Span::call_site())` in `self.app` when no app ident was written. Single-app users see no behavioural change; multi-app users now inherit from the server they actually registered against.
verification: grep -n 'Ident::new("APP"' hotaru_trans/src/url/urlargs.rs (no matches; only the existing fallback in urlexpr.rs remains)
